Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'S Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Sarah Adelaars, Esq. (650-638-0120) for Plaintiff Steve Mack
Barbara Cray, Esq. (650-654-2729) for Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., et al

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Denied as moot in light of the ruling on demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE GARRETT WONG, CLERK: VICKI MACK, NOT REPORTED.

Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Sarah Adelaars, Esq. (650-638-0120) for Plaintiff Steve Mack
Barbara Cray, Esq. (650-654-2729) for Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., et al

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. All causes of action are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations. A plaintiff has reason to know a potential claim exists when he has reason to suspect a factual basis for its elements. Suspicion of one or more of the elements of a cause of action, coupled with knowledge of any remaining elements, will generally trigger the statute of limitations period. (Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 807.) Further, "aggrieved parties generally need not know the exact manner in which their injuries were effected, nor the identities of all parties who may have played a role therein." (Bernson v. Browning-Ferris Indus. of Calif., Inc. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 926, 932.) The complaint pleads that Plaintiff knew in 2009 that Kier opened Wells Fargo accounts in his name without his authorization. (Complaint, par. 17.) The complaint also pleads that Plaintiff refused to provide written authorization for Kier to open a bank account in 2005 when Kier requested such information. (Complaint, par. 9.) Thus, the Wells Fargo documents that Plaintiff received in 2013 did not provide any information that Plaintiff did not already know in 2009; namely, that Kier opened accounts in his name without his authorization. The complaint was filed on February 10, 2014, more than four years after the discovery of the facts giving rise to all four causes of action. As such, the complaint is barred by the applicable two and three year statutes of limitations. (CCP secs. 338 and 339.) The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has not demonstrated the complaint can be amended to cure this defect. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE GARRETT WONG, CLERK: VICKI MACK, NOT REPORTED.
